attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who attacks early in stages, showing willingness to go in consecutive daily breaks regardless of terrain difficulty or previous day's efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with significant autonomy, minimal visible team setup required. Self-sufficient break attempts suggest role as team's designated opportunist rather than protected rider.
terrain_preference: Demonstrates versatility across terrain types, comfortable in breakaways on both hilly stage races and flatter parcours. Not terrain-dependent for break selection.
vulnerability: Struggles to stay away on flat terrain despite strong condition. Lacks finishing power to convert breakaway efforts into results when groups remain together late.
key_patterns: Multiple consecutive daily breakaway attempts across different races (minimum 2 documented instances)
Exceptional recovery ability enabling back-to-back break efforts despite previous day exertion
Early-stage aggression with opportunistic timing rather than selective strategic positioning
Strong condition insufficient to finish breaks on flatter terrain profiles
form_trajectory: neutral: Strong condition and recovery evident throughout 2018 season, but consistent pattern of failed break attempts suggests tactical approach not yielding results
